studies.title: Randomized controlled trial to evaluate the effectiveness of a digital health application (Kaia) to prevent back pain and improve quality of life in hospital staff

_verification.reason: The study specifically evaluates the effectiveness of the Kaia app, a digital health application for chronic back pain, in preventing back pain and improving quality of life among hospital staff. The product name 'Kaia' is mentioned multiple times in the trial description, and it is identified as a DiGA approved for chronic back pain.

_classification.reason: The study is explicitly described as a 'randomized controlled trial' which evaluates the effectiveness of a digital health application (Kaia) compared to standard prevention measures, indicating random allocation to intervention and control groups under controlled conditions.

studies.title: Kaia back pain for the treatment of non-specific low back pain in adult patients - a randomised controlled trial

_verification.reason: The study is specifically about the Kaia Back Pain app, which is a digital therapeutic product developed by Kaia Health Software GmbH. The product name 'Kaia Back Pain' appears in the title and throughout the full text, and the manufacturer is identified as the developer.

_classification.reason: Keyword match: 3 RCT indicators vs 0 RWE indicators.

studies.title: e-Health and mobile health application for the self-management support of patients with low back pain - a confirmatory trial

_verification.reason: The study specifically investigates the effectiveness of the 'Kaia' app for low back pain, which matches the digital therapeutic product 'Kaia Rückenschmerzen - Rückentraining für Zuhause'. The product name 'kaia' is mentioned multiple times in the full text, and the manufacturer 'Kaia Health Software GmbH' is listed as a contact.

_classification.reason: The study randomly allocates participants with low back pain to either an intervention group using a medical back app or a control group receiving standard physiotherapy and online information, which is characteristic of a randomized controlled trial.

studies.title: Innovative Back Pain Therapy with eHealth for our Patients

_verification.reason: The study specifically mentions that patients in the intervention group are treated with a back pain app by Kaia, which aligns with the product Kaia Rückenschmerzen - Rückentraining für Zuhause.
